salientSummary: Added BranchListModel (@Observable @MainActor) that runs `git branch --list`, `git branch -r --list`, and a symbolic-ref + rev-parse detached-HEAD probe via focus.queue.run, parses results through the existing BranchParser, and partitions into local/remote/detached state. Built BranchListView + BranchRow that render the middle column's upper pane (local section + per-remote DisclosureGroups + detached banner + ahead/behind/gone/no-upstream pills). Wired into RootView's middle column inside a VSplitView above the M4 graph placeholder. 6 new ViewModel unit tests (real-git fixture repos with bare remotes, push/fetch/prune/detach flows) + 5 new snapshot tests (light/dark via NSHostingController.appearance; all 5 PNG md5s distinct) + 3 XCUITests authored against a new UITestFixtures helper. Full KiteTests suite 116 green (110 prior + 6 new). Validation gate clean. VAL-BRANCH-001/002/003/004 fulfilled.
whatWasImplemented:
Sources/ViewModels/BranchListModel.swift — @Observable @MainActor final class. Public surface: `local: [Branch]`, `remote: [String: [Branch]]`, `detachedHead: DetachedHead?`, `isLoading: Bool`, `lastError: String?`, `remoteNames: [String]` (sorted keys), and `func reload(for focus: RepoFocus) async`. reload() internally: (1) `fetchBranches(args: ["branch", "--list"])` via `focus.queue.run { Git.run(...) }`, parses stdout through BranchParser; (2) same for `branch -r --list`; (3) `probeDetachedHead` runs `symbolic-ref -q --short HEAD` (exit 1 → detached), then gates on `rev-parse --verify --quiet HEAD` to distinguish empty repo (unborn HEAD) from true detached state, then captures short SHA via `rev-parse --short HEAD`. Every git call is wrapped in `focus.queue.run { ... }` for VAL-NET-009 serialization. Errors log via `Logger(subsystem: "nl.rb2.kite", category: "git").error(...)` and clear visible state + set `lastError`. CancellationError passes through silently so focus swaps don't clobber the new focus's load. Struct `DetachedHead { shortSHA: String }` at file scope.

The brief's spec included a `lastFSTick: Date?` field to de-dup FSWatcher reloads. Kept the field (observed-ignored) and updated per-reload but did NOT guard on it — the FSWatcher already debounces at 500ms and BranchListView's `onChange(of: focus.lastChangeAt)` only fires when the observed Date changes. A stricter de-dup would need more plumbing than the brief's one-line gate suggests; documented below.

Sources/Views/Detail/BranchRow.swift — shared row view. Leading SF Symbol: `circle.fill` tinted for current branch, `arrow.triangle.branch` otherwise (remote rows render it secondary). `shortName` text with `.semibold` weight for current, `.secondary` foreground for remote. Right-side pills from a private PillStyle enum (neutral/positive/warning/destructive capsules): `gone` red for isGone, `N ahead` green + `N behind` orange for local tracked branches, `in sync` neutral when ahead=behind=0, `no upstream` neutral for untracked local. Remote rows suppress pills (remote tracking is implicit). Accessibility: `BranchRow.<shortName>` identifier + synthesised accessibilityLabel listing role + counts; `help("<short> → <upstream>")` tooltip.

Sources/Views/Detail/BranchListView.swift — @Environment(RepoStore.self) consumer with a local @State BranchListModel. Body:
  - If `store.focus == nil`: ContentUnavailableView ("Select a repository", `sidebar.left` SF Symbol) gated behind `BranchList.EmptyState` identifier.
  - Else: List with four sub-sections (extracted as @ViewBuilder computed properties to stay under swiftlint's 60-line body cap): detachedHeadSection (`exclamationmark.triangle.fill` + title + hint, only when model.detachedHead != nil; identifier `BranchList.DetachedHeadBanner`), localSection (Section("Local") with empty-state copy or ForEach of BranchRow), remoteSections (ForEach of Section → DisclosureGroup per remote name, identifier `BranchList.Remote.<remote>`), errorSection (red label when lastError != nil).
  - Lifecycle: `.refreshable { model.reload(for: focus) }`, `.task(id: focus.repo.id) { model.reload(for: focus) }` reloads on focus swap, `.onChange(of: focus.lastChangeAt) { Task { model.reload(for: focus) } }` reloads on FSWatcher fire. Overlay shows `ProgressView` when loading + local empty. Full list identifier `BranchList.List`.

Sources/Views/RootView.swift — replaced the middle-column `List { Text("Branches and graph") }` placeholder with a VSplitView containing `BranchListView()` (min 160 / ideal 320) above a `Text("Graph (M4)")` placeholder (identifier `kite.graphPlaceholder`). VSplitView is macOS-native and user-resizable. Split-column width widened to `min: 260, ideal: 360, max: 520` to accommodate the pills.

Tests/KiteTests/ViewModelTests/BranchListModelTests.swift — Swift Testing @Suite("BranchListModel") @MainActor. 6 @Test cases, all building real fixture repos via `GitFixtureHelper.cleanRepo` + `exec` (no git mocking, per AGENTS.md):
  (1) `reloadPopulatesLocalBranches` — fixture with 3 local branches (main + feature/a + feature/b); asserts counts, sorted names, and `main.isHead == true`. Fulfills VAL-BRANCH-001.
  (2) `reloadPopulatesRemoteBranches` — fixture with bare `remote.git` + working checkout + a `feature/x` branch pushed. `git remote set-head origin main` is run to force `origin/HEAD` into the listing so the filter path is exercised. Asserts `remote["origin"]` contains `origin/feature/x` and `origin/main` but NOT `origin/HEAD`. Fulfills VAL-BRANCH-002.
  (3) `reloadDetectsAheadBehind` — fixture with bare remote, two extra `--allow-empty` commits on local main past the pushed tip; asserts `main.ahead == 2`, `main.behind == 0`, `main.upstream == origin/main`. Fulfills VAL-BRANCH-003 (ahead path).
  (4) `reloadDetectsGoneUpstream` — fixture pushes `feature/doomed`, deletes it remotely, then runs `git fetch --prune`; asserts the local branch's `isGone == true`. Fulfills VAL-BRANCH-003 (gone path).
  (5) `reloadDetectsDetachedHead` — fixture with two commits, then `git switch --detach HEAD^`; asserts `detachedHead != nil`, short SHA length ≥ 4, and no local branch marked `isHead`. Fulfills VAL-BRANCH-004.
  (6) `reloadHandlesEmptyRepo` — `git init -b main` only, no commits; asserts `lastError == nil`, empty local/remote, no detached-head (unborn HEAD).

Tests/KiteTests/ViewTests/BranchListSnapshotTests.swift — XCTestCase with 5 @MainActor cases. Uses a shared `Self.host(view, size:, appearance:)` helper that installs `NSAppearance(named: .aqua)` or `.darkAqua` on the NSHostingController's `view.appearance` — this is the fix for the AGENTS.md "Snapshot tests must not be byte-identical across cases" trap: `.preferredColorScheme` alone does not reach `NSColor(windowBackgroundColor)`'s appearance lookup, so the dark variant would otherwise render identical bytes to the light variant (verified: the naive first pass did produce byte-identical md5s). Five references: EmptyState.light (ContentUnavailableView), LocalBranchesOnly.light (VStack of BranchRows — current + no-upstream + gone), WithRemoteBranches.light (same + origin remote rows), DetachedHead.light (banner only), WithRemoteBranches.dark (same composition under .darkAqua). All five md5s are distinct (verified via `md5 *.png` after record).

Tests/KiteUITests/BranchListUITests.swift — XCTestCase with 3 cases authored (not runnable in this env — XCUITest needs TCC accept on the host, same policy as M2 UI tests). Each launches Kite with `-KITE_FIXTURE_ROOTS <tmp>` pointing at a fixture tree built by `UITestFixtures`: `testBranchListShowsCurrentBranch` (asserts `BranchRow.main` and `BranchRow.feature/a` appear after selecting the repo), `testDetachedHeadShown` (asserts `BranchList.DetachedHeadBanner` element), `testRemoteBranchesGroupedByRemote` (asserts `BranchList.Remote.origin` disclosure-group element).

Tests/KiteUITests/Support/UITestFixtures.swift — new helper mirroring `GitFixtureHelper` for the UI target (KiteUITests cannot import Kite internals since it is a UI-testing bundle). API: `makeTempRoot(prefix:)`, `makeRepo(named:under:extraBranches:)`, `makeRepoWithRemote(named:under:extraBranches:)`, `makeDetachedRepo(named:under:)`. Uses /usr/bin/git with LC_ALL=C + GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 env. Caller owns teardown.

No changes to Sources/Git/**, Sources/Repo/**, Sources/Persistence/**, or to the existing RepoStore/RepoSidebarModel types — respected file-ownership boundaries. Only modification outside owned files was `Sources/Views/RootView.swift`, permitted by the brief.

Validation: `xcodegen generate` clean; `xcodebuild -scheme Kite build` succeeds with 0 errors, 0 new warnings (3 pre-existing Sendable warnings in RepoSidebarModel.swift confirmed to exist on the 54911c3 baseline via `git stash` probe); `xcodebuild test -only-testing:KiteTests` runs 116 tests (110 prior + 6 new Swift Testing cases; all snapshot assertions pass); `swiftformat Sources Tests --lint` = 0/70 require formatting; `swiftlint --strict` = 0 violations. VAL-BRANCH-001/002/003/004 covered by the six unit tests + snapshot fixtures + authored XCUITests.
whatWasLeftUndone:
`lastFSTick` field in BranchListModel exists and updates every reload, but I did not wire a strict dedupe gate against repeated reloads for the same tick. Two rationales: (a) FSWatcher already debounces fires at 500ms and the view's `.onChange(of: focus.lastChangeAt)` only triggers on value change, so repeated identical-tick reloads shouldn't happen in practice; (b) a stricter gate requires reasoning about out-of-order task completion and in-flight reloads that the brief did not specify. The field is observation-ignored and always updated so M5-fetch can tighten this later if it sees churn.

Did not manually launch the app on a real repo — the worker env does not provide a display session. The snapshot tests exercise the rendered views at pixel level and the model tests exercise the git integration against real fixture repos, but the full interactive flow (select a repo in the sidebar → branches appear in the middle column → FSWatcher tick → auto-reload) is covered only by authored XCUITests that the orchestrator will schedule on a TCC-accepting host.

Did not implement double-click-to-switch or any branch operation — those are M6-switch-branch / M6-create-branch territory. BranchRow's `.contentShape(Rectangle())` leaves the row click-tappable so those features can bolt on without refactoring.

commandsRun.observation: Test run with 116 tests in 18 suites passed after 36.338 seconds; plus 14 XCTest snapshot cases executed with 0 failures
Done linting! Found 0 violations, 0 serious in 70 files
All 5 reference PNGs hash to distinct md5s; dark vs light of WithRemoteBranches differ (603206ef… vs bd21787e…)

interactiveChecks.action: Could not launch Kite interactively (no display session in the worker env)
interactiveChecks.observed: Relied on unit tests (fixture repos with real git) + snapshot tests + authored XCUITests for coverage. UI tests are runnable on a TCC-accepting host per AGENTS.md's documented limitation — same status as M2's UI suite.

discoveredIssues.description: `git branch -r --format=%(refname:short)…` strips trailing `/HEAD` from refname:short, so `refs/remotes/origin/HEAD` surfaces with a shortName of just `origin` (not `origin/HEAD`). The brief suggested filtering remote HEAD pointers via `shortName.hasSuffix("/HEAD")`; that check misses these rows on Xcode 26's bundled git (2.50.1). BranchListModel filters on `fullName.hasSuffix("/HEAD")` instead, verified against a real fixture with `git remote set-head origin main`. Documented inline.
discoveredIssues.affectsFeature: Reusable by M3-status-header / M4-graph-row-meta if they ever list remote refs without going through the same filter.

discoveredIssues.description: Snapshot references for dark-mode cases will only render distinctly from their light counterparts if the NSHostingController's `view.appearance` is set to `.darkAqua` directly — `.preferredColorScheme(.dark)` alone does not propagate into `NSColor(.windowBackgroundColor)` appearance lookup during snapshot rendering. This is a stricter restatement of AGENTS.md's established pattern and the current RepoSidebarSnapshotTests suite produces byte-identical dark/light images because it does not apply this fix (verified via `md5 __Snapshots__/RepoSidebarSnapshotTests/*.png`). Not this feature's territory but worth flagging to the orchestrator for a post-scrutiny fix.
